config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan সমাজ terlalu mudah dipakai untuk mengalahkan individu, itu bisa membuka jalan ke penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat perlindungan hak dan keadilan, bukan dengan mengorbankan orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public safety, fraud prevention, and basic order sometimes justify limits. The key difference is that those limits should be narrow, clearly defined, and subject to strong legal protections, not a blank check for “the common good.” To me, a healthy society is one that protects the individual first and then justifies any restriction with real necessity, not vague appeals to the collective.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diutamakan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak proporsional, bahkan kadang menutup kritik dan melindungi pihak yang kuat. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti keamanan publik atau bencana, tetapi pembatasan harus ketat, sementara, dan bisa diuji, bukan jadi alasan untuk menginjak hak orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika hak individu dihormati, lalu kepentingan bersama dibangun tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should take priority over individual rights as a general principle, because in the United States that logic has repeatedly been used to justify censorship, segregation, and other abuses. The argument for the collective sounds appealing in the abstract, but once you let it override rights too easily, the people with power decide whose rights matter and whose do not. At the same time, I do not think rights are a license to ignore public safety or basic civic rules, so some limits are legitimate when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable. For me, the burden should always be on society to prove why a restriction is justified, not on the individual to prove why a right deserves protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi selimut untuk keputusan yang tidak transparan, pembatasan berlebihan, atau suara warga kecil yang diabaikan. Memang ada keadaan darurat atau keamanan publik yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi itu harus benar-benar sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling gampang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa merampas martabat dan hak orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that idea has too often been a cover for censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. The strongest point the other side makes is that emergencies and public safety are real, but that does not justify turning “the common good” into a blank check. In my view, the burden has to stay on society to prove a narrow, necessary, and accountable reason for any limit on rights. A healthy society protects individuals first and builds the common good around those protections, not by sacrificing them whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
